text:
Market today: The mood is mixed but leaning positive for your tech book. Easing consumer inflation (CPI at 3.5%, less than expected) is a tailwind, reducing pressure on the Fed. However, hawkish Fed comments from Warsh suggest vigilance against inflation remains.

Your holdings: SOXL (-29.35%, SELL) and SPAL (-30.91%, NEW) need immediate attention due to their leveraged nature and significant drawdowns. PTNM (-15.25%, NEW) is a tiny spec with liquidity concerns. Margin amplifies these drops.

Watch: Keep a close eye on further inflation data and any shifts in Fed tone. Also, monitor your leveraged positions, especially SOXL and SPAL, against their stops.

Bottom line: Prioritize managing risk on your leveraged, underperforming positions while watching for continued disinflationary trends.
plays: Consider trimming SOXL (-29.35%) given its SELL status and leveraged decay; respect its stop at 200.
Monitor SPAL (-30.91%) closely as a new, fast-moving leveraged ETF; respect its stop at 21.38.
Scanner shows a strong long setup for NVDA (entry 205.12-204.45, stop 203) if you're looking to add to chips.
